diagnoses.diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
diagnoses.clinical_rationale: HbA1c 6.3% and fasting glucose 118 mg/dL confirm pre-diabetes. HOMA-IR 8.3 (ref <2.0) with fasting insulin 28.4 µIU/mL confirms severe insulin resistance. Adiponectin critically low at 3.2 µg/mL (ref 5-30) and leptin markedly elevated at 32.5 ng/mL indicate adipose-driven hormonal dysfunction. Triglycerides 290 mg/dL with HDL 34 mg/dL complete metabolic syndrome criteria. Truck driver lifestyle (sedentary, fast food, irregular sleep, smoking) and strong family history of T2D/MI create high progression risk.

diagnoses.diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
diagnoses.clinical_rationale: Severely atherogenic lipid profile: LDL 168 mg/dL, ApoB 148 mg/dL (ref <90), LDL particle number 1680 nmol/L (ref <1000), Pattern B with peak size 20.2 Å (small-dense particles most atherogenic). TC/HDL ratio 7.6 (ref <5.0). Triglycerides 290 mg/dL compounding cardiovascular risk. Homocysteine 16.8 µmol/L adds independent vascular risk. Father had MI at 58 — patient is 44 with this profile and no treatment.

diagnoses.diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3 and Androgen Decline
diagnoses.clinical_rationale: TSH 5.8 uIU/mL (ref 0.4-4.0) with low-normal T4 Free (0.9) and low T3 Free (2.1 pg/mL, ref 2.3-4.2) indicates subclinical hypothyroidism impairing cellular metabolism. Testosterone total 215 ng/dL and free 4.2 pg/mL are both below normal. DHEA-S 118 µg/dL (ref 160-449) low. Elevated morning cortisol (22.5 µg/dL) reflects chronic stress from irregular sleep/lifestyle. IGF-1 105 ng/mL below range. Vitamin D severely deficient at 14 ng/mL — deficiency worsens hypothyroidism and insulin resistance.

foods_to_eat.food: Fatty fish — salmon, sardines, mackerel (salmón, sardinas, caballa)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: EPA/DHA directly lower triglycerides (currently 290 mg/dL), shift LDL toward larger less-atherogenic particles, and reduce inflammation. Critical for your Pattern B lipid profile.

foods_to_eat.food: Black beans, lentils, chickpeas (frijoles negros, lentejas, garbanzos)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Soluble fiber slows glucose absorption, reduces blood sugar spikes, and lowers LDL cholesterol. High fiber foods directly improve HOMA-IR over time.

foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Monounsaturated fats raise HDL (currently low at 34 mg/dL) and help shift LDL toward larger, less harmful particles.

foods_to_eat.food: Extra virgin olive oil (aceite de oliva extra virgen)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Replaces inflammatory fats from fast food. Polyphenols reduce oxidation of LDL particles and lower hs-CRP.

foods_to_eat.food: Broccoli, cauliflower, Brussels sprouts (brócoli, coliflor, coles)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Lightly cooked cruciferous vegetables support liver detoxification important for T3 conversion. Sulforaphane improves insulin sensitivity.

foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3 and Androgen Decline
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Zinc and magnesium support thyroid hormone conversion (T4 to T3) and testosterone synthesis. Zinc is often deficient in smokers.

foods_to_eat.food: Eggs from pasture-raised hens (huevos de gallinas libres)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3 and Androgen Decline
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Excellent source of choline, selenium (essential for T4-to-T3 conversion), and iodine. The complete amino acid profile supports testosterone synthesis.

foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Omega-3 ALA and polyphenols improve LDL particle size and reduce cardiovascular inflammation.

foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Beta-glucan fiber binds to cholesterol and bile acids in the gut, reducing LDL by 5-10%. Also slows glucose absorption.

foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Improves insulin sensitivity and reduces post-meal blood sugar spikes. Studies show HbA1c reduction with regular use.

foods_to_eat.food: Turmeric with black pepper (cúrcuma con pimienta negra)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Curcumin reduces hs-CRP (currently 4.8 mg/L), activates AMPK improving insulin sensitivity, and supports liver function for T3 conversion.

foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3 and Androgen Decline
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Highest dietary source of selenium, essential for thyroid hormone conversion and testicular function. 2-3 nuts provides the full daily requirement.

foods_to_eat.food: Leafy greens — spinach, Swiss chard (espinaca, acelga)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Magnesium-rich foods support insulin signaling. Your serum magnesium is low at 1.5 mg/dL. Also provides nitrates supporting blood vessel health.

foods_to_eat.food: Green plantains, cooked and cooled (plátano verde cocido y enfriado)
foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Resistant starch feeds beneficial gut bacteria and improves insulin sensitivity. Accessible staple across Latin America.

foods_to_eat.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_eat.why_it_helps: Allicin reduces LDL, total cholesterol, and homocysteine levels. Your homocysteine is elevated at 16.8 µmol/L.

foods_to_avoid.food: Fast food and ultra-processed foods (comida rápida, ultraprocesados)
foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Your primary dietary risk. Fast food is the single largest driver of your insulin resistance, elevated triglycerides, and atherogenic lipid pattern. Complete elimination is essential.

foods_to_avoid.food: Sweetened beverages — sodas, energy drinks (refrescos, bebidas energéticas)
foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Fructose directly raises triglycerides and worsens HOMA-IR. Your triglycerides at 290 mg/dL are very high.

foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Smoking oxidizes LDL particles, making Pattern B even more atherogenic. It also lowers testosterone and worsens thyroid function. Cessation is medically urgent.

foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Alcohol directly raises triglycerides and your ALT/AST are both elevated. Complete elimination for at least 3 months.

foods_to_avoid.food: Refined carbohydrates — white bread, white rice, tortillas de harina
foods_to_avoid.reduction_target: Maximum 1 serving daily; replace with whole grains or legumes
foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Rapidly spike blood sugar and insulin. With HOMA-IR of 8.3, your cells are severely resistant — every blood sugar spike accelerates damage.

foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Creates AGEs and oxidized lipids that worsen LDL oxidation and raise hs-CRP further.

foods_to_avoid.food: Processed meats — bacon, sausage, hot dogs (tocino, salchicha, hot dogs)
foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Saturated fat and nitrates worsen cardiovascular risk. Your Pattern B LDL is already highly atherogenic.

foods_to_avoid.food: Vegetable oils high in omega-6 (aceite de maíz, soya)
foods_to_avoid.reduction_target: Replace entirely with olive oil or avocado oil
foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Excess omega-6 worsens inflammation and insulin resistance. Your hs-CRP is 4.8 mg/L.

foods_to_avoid.food: Commercial sweet breads and pastries (pan dulce, pasteles)
foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Combine refined flour, sugar, and trans fats — maximally harmful for your metabolic profile.

foods_to_avoid.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
foods_to_avoid.why_to_avoid: Saturated fat from red meat raises LDL and worsen Pattern B. Prioritize fish and plant protein.

exercise_recommendations.duration: Start with 15-20 min, build to 45 min over 8 weeks
exercise_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
exercise_recommendations.why_it_helps: As a truck driver with near-zero activity, any movement is high-impact. Post-meal walking reduces blood sugar spikes 20-30% and begins addressing HOMA-IR. This is the most accessible starting point given your occupation.
exercise_recommendations.safety_notes: You have no known cardiovascular diagnosis but very high risk profile. If you experience chest pain or severe shortness of breath, stop and seek care immediately.

exercise_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3 and Androgen Decline
exercise_recommendations.why_it_helps: Heavy compound resistance training (squats, deadlifts, presses) stimulates testosterone production and improves thyroid conversion. Building muscle increases glucose receptors, directly reducing HOMA-IR.
exercise_recommendations.safety_notes: Begin with moderate weights. Avoid Valsalva maneuver given likely unmanaged hypertension.

exercise_recommendations.exercise_type: Zone 2 Cardio — cycling, swimming, or fast walking
exercise_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
exercise_recommendations.why_it_helps: Sustained moderate cardio raises HDL (currently 34 mg/dL), lowers triglycerides, and shifts LDL particle size over time. Most effective for the lipid profile.
exercise_recommendations.safety_notes: Given family history of MI and current cardiometabolic risk, stress test may be advisable before starting vigorous cardio. Consult physician.

supplement_recommendations.dosage_range: 5,000 IU Vitamin D3 + 100-200 mcg K2 (MK-7) daily
supplement_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3 and Androgen Decline
supplement_recommendations.why_it_helps: Vitamin D at 14 ng/mL is severely deficient — this worsens insulin resistance, thyroid function, and testosterone production simultaneously. K2 ensures calcium is directed to bones not arteries.
supplement_recommendations.safety_note: No medications to interact with. Retest in 10-12 weeks.
supplement_recommendations.local_availability_note: Vitamin D3 widely available in Latin American pharmacies. K2 available in health food stores.

supplement_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Atherogenic Dyslipidemia with Small-Dense LDL Pattern B
supplement_recommendations.why_it_helps: At high doses, directly lowers triglycerides 25-30% (currently 290 mg/dL — very high). Also shifts LDL particles toward larger size and reduces hs-CRP.
supplement_recommendations.local_availability_note: Widely available in Latin American pharmacies.

supplement_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
supplement_recommendations.why_it_helps: Activates AMPK, improving insulin sensitivity at the cellular level. Also shown to lower LDL, triglycerides, and HbA1c. Your HOMA-IR of 8.3 and HbA1c of 6.3% indicate this will provide significant benefit.
supplement_recommendations.safety_note: No current medications. Monitor for low blood sugar symptoms. Well tolerated.
supplement_recommendations.local_availability_note: Available in health food stores (tiendas naturistas) across Latin America.

supplement_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Metabolic Syndrome with Severe Insulin Resistance
supplement_recommendations.why_it_helps: Serum magnesium is low at 1.5 mg/dL. Magnesium is essential for insulin signaling, blood sugar metabolism, thyroid function, and sleep quality — all of which are compromised in your profile.
supplement_recommendations.local_availability_note: Magnesium Citrate available in most pharmacies; Glycinate form at health food stores.

supplement_recommendations.target_diagnosis: Subclinical Hypothyroidism with Low Free T3 and Androgen Decline
supplement_recommendations.why_it_helps: Selenium is required for deiodinase enzymes that convert T4 to active T3. Your T3 Free is below range. Selenium also supports testicular function and reduces thyroid antibody activity.
supplement_recommendations.safety_note: Do not exceed 400 mcg/day. If eating 2-3 Brazil nuts daily, reduce supplemental dose to 100 mcg.
supplement_recommendations.local_availability_note: Selenium supplements available in pharmacies and health food stores throughout Latin America.

patient_summary: Your lab panel reviewed 67 markers across 6 panels, with 33 values outside normal range — one of the highest-burden profiles evaluated. The core issue is severe insulin resistance (HOMA-IR 8.3, fasting insulin 28.4 µIU/mL) with pre-diabetes (HbA1c 6.3%, fasting glucose 118 mg/dL), an extremely atherogenic lipid profile featuring small-dense LDL Pattern B particles, triglycerides of 290 mg/dL, and HDL of only 34 mg/dL. Your thyroid is underperforming (TSH 5.8, T3 Free below range), testosterone levels are below normal, and vitamin D is severely deficient at 14 ng/mL — these hormonal deficiencies amplify the metabolic dysfunction. Smoking, daily fast food, alcohol use, and irregular sleep are actively driving every abnormal value in your panel. The most impactful immediate steps are quitting smoking, eliminating fast food and alcohol, starting daily walking, and beginning Vitamin D3, omega-3 fish oil, and berberine supplementation. Without intervention, your current trajectory leads to Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease within 3-5 years — but these markers are fully reversible with consistent lifestyle change. Comprehensive repeat labs at 3 months are strongly recommended, along with physician evaluation for thyroid and cardiovascular risk management.

system_summaries.summary: Complete blood count is within normal limits. No anemia or immune abnormalities detected.

Kidney function is normal (creatinine 1.0, BUN 19). Magnesium is low at 1.5 mg/dL — a deficiency that worsens insulin resistance, thyroid function, and blood pressure. Dietary magnesium and supplementation are recommended.

Liver enzymes are significantly elevated: ALT 72 U/L and AST 64 U/L indicate liver cell stress. This pattern is consistent with metabolic fatty liver disease driven by insulin resistance, high triglycerides, and dietary factors. Alcohol use and smoking compound the damage. Dietary changes, alcohol elimination, and liver-supportive supplements are essential.

Your metabolic panel reveals severe insulin resistance. HOMA-IR of 8.3 is more than 4x the upper limit. HbA1c 6.3% and glucose 118 mg/dL confirm pre-diabetes. Critically low adiponectin (3.2 µg/mL) and high leptin (32.5 ng/mL) indicate adipose tissue is actively driving metabolic disease. Every dietary and lifestyle intervention in this plan targets these markers directly.

Your cardiovascular risk profile is severely elevated. ApoB 148 mg/dL, LDL 168 mg/dL, LDL particle number 1680, and Pattern B (small-dense) lipids indicate maximum atherogenic risk. TC/HDL ratio 7.6 (ref <5.0) and triglycerides 290 mg/dL compound the risk. No statin or medication is currently prescribed. Physician evaluation for cardiovascular risk management is urgently recommended alongside lifestyle intervention.

hs-CRP at 4.8 mg/L indicates high systemic inflammation driving cardiovascular risk. Homocysteine at 16.8 µmol/L is elevated — a marker of vascular damage risk that can be reduced with B vitamins. Smoking is a major driver of both.

TSH is elevated at 5.8 uIU/mL with low Free T3 (2.1 pg/mL), indicating subclinical hypothyroidism. Thyroid antibodies are negative, suggesting this is functional rather than autoimmune. Low thyroid function slows metabolism, worsens cholesterol, promotes weight gain, and reduces testosterone. Selenium and vitamin D repletion may improve T3 conversion; physician evaluation is recommended.

Testosterone (total 215 ng/dL, free 4.2 pg/mL) and DHEA-S (118 µg/dL) are both below normal, consistent with androgen decline accelerated by obesity, insulin resistance, and chronic stress. Morning cortisol is elevated (22.5 µg/dL) reflecting HPA axis activation from irregular sleep and lifestyle stress. IGF-1 is low at 105 ng/mL. PSA % Free at 22% is borderline low — physician follow-up recommended. Resistance training, sleep optimization, and metabolic improvements will help restore androgen levels.

Vitamin D is severely deficient at 14 ng/mL — urgent repletion needed as it affects insulin sensitivity, thyroid function, testosterone, and immune regulation simultaneously. B12 and folate are adequate; however, elevated homocysteine (16.8 µmol/L) suggests methylation inefficiency that may benefit from methylated B vitamins.
